posts.title: 📬 PVHS Weekly Newsletter 4/2/2026
posts.summary: Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter! Spring Break begins 4/3, guest speaker Erica Alfaro on 4/13, and CAASPP testing starts 4/16.
posts.author: Araceli Mandujano-Padilla
posts.body:
Hello Panthers

Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter! We're excited to share the latest happenings and updates from our school community to keep you informed and engaged.

Paul R. Robinson, Ph.D. - Principal

Spring Break
Spring Break is here! There will be no classes from Friday 4/3/26 to Friday 4/10/26. We return on Monday 4/13/26. As we head into Spring Break, I want to wish all of our students and staff a well-deserved time to rest, recharge, and enjoy the season. Whether you're traveling, spending time with family and friends, or simply taking a pause from the busy school routine, I hope this break brings you joy, renewed energy, and moments of reflection. Thank you for all the hard work you put into making our school a strong, supportive community — I look forward to welcoming everyone back refreshed and ready for a strong finish to the school year. Enjoy your break and take care.

Guest Speaker 4/13
Motivational speaker Erica Alfaro will be presenting at PVHS on Monday 4/13/26 at 6pm in the PVHS Library. PVHS, in partnership with TKJHS, is very excited to offer our School Community a chance to hear an inspirational message from Erica Alfaro. Erica is an award-winning author, keynote speaker, and educational advocate. In 2019, Erica's graduation pictures went viral and made headlines around the world. She was featured in major media outlets such as CNN, The Huffington Post, and Yahoo News. Erica's Harvesting Dreams book is an empowering, inspirational and motivational story. It is a testimony of a girl who went through many adversities and managed to reinvent herself and become the woman she is today. Erica's book won the 2022 International Latino Book Award in the Most Inspirational Young Adult category. Don't miss this wonderful opportunity to be inspired! Watch the 1-minute intro video below!

The Rotary Club Essay Contest
Congratulations to Zaira Nacar for submitting a winning essay in the Santa Maria Breakfast Rotary's 4-Way Test Essay Contest. We are proud of her accomplishment, and she will have the honor of reading her winning essay to the Rotary group on April 30th.

Merrie Okie-Goldin - Assistant Principal, Special Programs

CAASPP Testing Begins April 16
When we return from break, our CAASPP state testing window will begin, starting with Science (CAST) on April 16. Please see the testing calendar below for specific dates and grade-level schedules. Today, Ben Alberry, Izzy Hernandez, and I visited all 11th-grade English classes in the PAC to share a "You're a Smart Cookie" presentation. The discussion focused on why these tests matter and the importance of students giving their best effort. For juniors, CAASPP results are more than just a state requirement — they can have real benefits after high school. Strong performance on the 11th-grade CAASPP English and Math assessments may be used by California community colleges and CSU campuses as part of college course placement. Students who meet or exceed the standard may be able to start in college-level courses, which can save time and money by avoiding additional non-credit or support classes.

  • Get a good night's sleep
  • Eat breakfast
  • Take their time
  • Show what they know

Thank you for your continued support as we help our students prepare for success — both now and after graduation. A very special thank you to our testing staff, Ben Alberry and Izzy Hernandez, for doing 6 period presentations in a row with me! Have a wonderful spring break!

Jeffrey Hromadka - Assistant Principal, Student Services

Senior Event
PVHS wrapped up the week with an exciting and eventful day! Seniors gathered for a senior meeting, captured memories with a panoramic picture, and received their graduation gowns — all hosted by Mrs. Walters.

Kevin Ilac - Assistant Principal, Instruction

Junior Financial Aid Presentations
Our counselors met with all 11th grade students this week to highlight the importance of applying for financial aid. A detailed and informative presentation was shared with the junior class to help guide them through the process. We thank our staff and students for their time and engagement, and for allowing valuable class time to be used to further their understanding of financial aid opportunities.

Spring Faire
We celebrated today with our annual Spring Faire, where our clubs came together to sell items and fundraise for their organizations. Thank you to our students and staff for making the day fun-filled, productive, and seamless. We truly appreciate everyone's efforts. Have a wonderful and restful Spring Break!

Christian Basulto - Dean of Students

BLE Cycle Assessment
As part of the next step in the Curriculum Alignment Project (CAP), the Biology of the Living Earth (BLE) team from all three comprehensive sites came together this week to begin creating cycle assessments. Using the platforms Inner Orbit and Gemini, teachers collaborated to carefully design questions that included a balanced mix of 1D, 2D, and 3D assessment items, ensuring that students are being evaluated on content knowledge, application, and deeper scientific thinking. This collaborative process helps strengthen curriculum alignment and consistency across all BLE classrooms at Pioneer Valley, Santa Maria, and Righetti. Great work to our Biology and Ag Biology teachers for their continued commitment to providing high-quality and equitable learning experiences for all students! Lastly, I want to wish you all a well-deserved and relaxing Spring Break! Be safe and enjoy the time off, Panthers!

posts.title: 🎓 Meet with an Allan Hancock College Counselor at PVHS Tuesday, April 21st
posts.summary: Hey Panthers! If you're planning on attending Allan Hancock College in the fall, scan the QR code to reserve your appointment for Tuesday, April 21st. You must meet with an AHC Counselor.
posts.author: Melani Mederos
posts.body:
Hey Panthers!

If you're planning on attending Allan Hancock College in the fall, and you haven't met with an AHC Counselor yet, scan the QR code below to reserve your appointment time for Tuesday, April 21st.

You must meet with an AHC Counselor to get your "First Semester Plan" so you can register for classes in May!

Questions? Please see your PVHS Counselor, Mr. Hernandez in the library, or Mrs. Mederos in the College & Career Center (room 604).

posts.title: 🚨 Campus Update
posts.summary: Good Afternoon PV School Community. About noon today, a male PVHS student had a medical emergency during class. A school nurse immediately responded and the student was transported by ambulance.
posts.author: Paul Robinson
posts.body:
Good Afternoon PV School Community,

About noon today, a male PVHS student had a medical emergency during class. A school nurse immediately responded to the classroom and the student was transported by ambulance to the hospital for followup. Counseling services are always made available during these types of concerning situations.

Respectfully,
Paul Robinson, Principal

posts.title: 🎪 Spring Fair, Minimum Day, & Important Senior Updates! Tomorrow 4/2
posts.summary: We have an action-packed day tomorrow heading into Spring Break! Minimum day ends at 1:35 PM, Spring Fair at lunch, and major senior milestones during Periods 3 and 4.
posts.author: Jeffrey Hromadka
posts.body:
Dear Families and Students,

We have an action-packed day tomorrow as we head into our Spring Break! Please review the following updates regarding tomorrow's schedule and upcoming events.

🕒 1. Minimum Day Schedule
Tomorrow will be a minimum day. School will conclude after Period 7 at 1:35 PM. Please ensure transportation arrangements are made for this earlier dismissal time.

🎡 2. Spring Fair Tomorrow
Get ready for some fun! We are hosting our Spring Fair tomorrow during lunch. Our school clubs will be out in force selling a variety of food and treats. It's going to be a great day to enjoy the outdoors and support our student organizations.

🎓 3. Essential Updates for Seniors (Class of 2026)
Tomorrow is a major milestone day for our seniors! Activities will take place during Periods 3 and 4:

  • Panoramic Picture: Seniors will head to the stands for their official group photo.
  • Graduation Rehearsal: We will conduct our first graduation walkthrough.
  • Cap & Gown Distribution: Jostens will be on-site to hand out graduation gowns immediately following the photo.

Attendance is critical. We want every senior to be included in the picture and to receive their graduation gear on time!

🏖️ 4. Spring Break Reminder
Spring Break officially begins this Friday, April 3 (No School). We hope everyone enjoys a relaxing and safe break. Classes will resume on Monday, April 13.

posts.title: 📬 PVHS Weekly Newsletter 3/27/2026
posts.summary: Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter! Fiddler on the Roof at Clark Center, ELPAC testing wrapping up, and Spring Fair next Thursday.
posts.author: Araceli Mandujano-Padilla
posts.body:
Hello Panthers

Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter! We're excited to share the latest happenings and updates from our school community to keep you informed and engaged.

Paul R. Robinson, Ph.D. - Principal

Fiddler on the Roof
PVHS Center Stage rocked the Clark Center with their opening night performance of Fiddler on the Roof. Guest Artist J.J. Niemann, who has performed in Hamilton and other Broadway productions, joined PVHS performers for a set after the play to the delight of the crowd. PVHS Band Director and select students performed in the Orchestra pit in a wonderful collaboration between Theatre and Band. Thank you to Theatre Director Selyn Harwin and Band Director Lizbet Barriga Chavez for their hard work and dedication to give our students a memorable opening night. Upcoming performances include March 27 at 7:00pm and March 28 at 2:00pm and 7:00pm at the Clark Center, as well as April 16, 17, and 18 at 7:00pm in the Performing Arts Center. KSBY Coverage

Hiring Update
PVHS has hired two International Language (Spanish Teachers), and one Science teacher. We still have multiple Special Education Mild Moderate (MMSN) openings. If you know someone that is interested, please share our job posting.

Merrie Okie-Goldin - Assistant Principal, Special Programs

ELPAC Testing Reminder
We are wrapping up our ELPAC testing, and we still need a few students to complete their test on Monday, Tuesday, or Wednesday. If you have not yet tested, and have received a reminder phone call, please stop by and see Ms. Goldin as soon as possible. The ELPAC (English Language Proficiency Assessments for California) measures how well students listen, speak, read, and write in English. This test is very important because it helps ensure students receive the right support and services to be successful in school. Every student's participation matters. Thank you for helping us finish strong!

Jeffrey Hromadka - Assistant Principal, Student Services
Kick off your Spring Break with a bang at our Spring Fair next Thursday during lunch! It's the perfect time to celebrate the upcoming holiday while supporting our amazing school clubs. Be sure to bring some extra cash to enjoy the variety of food and snacks available while you soak up the pre-break festivities with friends. In addition to the fair, our seniors will gather for the Senior Graduation Assembly during periods 3 and 4. After reporting to class for attendance, seniors will head to the gym to receive vital end-of-year updates and — for those who have already ordered — pick up their caps and gowns. Most importantly, we'll be taking the official panoramic and class year photos, so come dressed to impress!

Kevin Ilac - Assistant Principal, Instruction
Our school counselors and Career Center Specialist recently visited 9th, 10th, and 11th grade classrooms to help students navigate the CCGI (California Colleges Guidance Initiative) digital platform. Grade-specific milestones:

  • 9th Grade: Students completed assessments to match their unique interests and skills with potential career paths.
  • 10th Grade: Sophomores reviewed previous results, completed an intelligence assessment, and began defining specific career goals.
  • 11th Grade: Juniors took the next step by aligning their saved careers with college majors and researching campuses that offer those programs.

Today, PVHS hosted the ASVAB military aptitude test with over 25 students in attendance. This week, Melani Mederos, College & Career Center Specialist, was honored by the U.S. Army Gold Coast Recruiting Company for her outstanding partnership and support.

Christian Basulto - Dean of Students

Diablo Canyon Nuclear Power Plant
On March 25th, students from Mr. Magni's AP Environmental Science class had the unique opportunity to visit the Diablo Canyon Nuclear Power Plant, located just north of Avila Beach. This field trip was especially meaningful, as the facility has not allowed student visits for the past few years and has only recently reopened its doors to schools. During the visit, students were able to engage directly with scientists and support staff who help operate a plant that provides nearly 10% of California's power. A special thank you to Mr. Magni, Ms. Mihelic, and Mrs. Knowlson for arranging and supporting this amazing field trip!

posts.title: 🏈 Mandatory Football Meeting for 9th Graders
posts.summary: Calling all 9th graders ready to suit up for football next season! Mandatory meeting on Tuesday, March 31 at 12:35 PM in Room 551. Bring your lunch and your "A" game.
posts.author: Jeffrey Hromadka
posts.body:
Calling all 9th graders ready to suit up for football next season! If you want to hit the field next fall, this is the meeting you can't miss (meaning it's mandatory).

  • When: Tuesday, March 31
  • Time: 12:35 PM (Don't be late!)
  • Where: Room 551 (Right by the cafeteria)
  • What to bring: Your lunch and your "A" game.

Let's get the season started right. See you there!

Coach Payne

posts.title: 🎓 Senior Meeting - next Thursday, April 2
posts.summary: CLASS OF 2026! Next Thursday, April 2, there will be a senior class meeting starting during 3rd period. Panoramic photos, graduation info, and cap & gown pickup for those who ordered.
posts.author: Lisa Walters
posts.body:
CLASS OF 2026!!

Next Thursday, April 2, there will be a senior class meeting, beginning during 3rd period. You will go to your 3rd period class and then be dismissed to the gym.

During the meeting, you will have your panoramic and class year pictures taken, will receive information about end of the year events, and if you have already ordered your cap and gown — you will pick it up at the end of the meeting.

Information about the distribution of the pictures will be shared after the meeting.

If you haven't ordered your cap and gown, please go to jostens.com to order.

Reminder — caps cannot be decorated or in any way modified.

posts.title: 🎓 College Craze Thursday
posts.summary: Tomorrow is College Craze Thursday! Remember to wear your COLLEGE gear! Teacher Spotlight: Mr. Cornejo — Modern World History & Ancient Medieval History — attended Cal State Fullerton.
posts.author: Rocio Trujillo
posts.body:
Tomorrow is College Craze Thursday!

Remember to wear your COLLEGE gear!

Teacher Spotlight
Mr. Cornejo
Teaches: Modern World History & Ancient Medieval History
Attended: California State University, Fullerton

Fun college memory: My favorite college memories were hosting my own radio show through Titan Radio on campus as well as studying in Florence, Italy for a semester. Being introduced to new experiences and cultures is something I hope all of our students have the opportunity to pursue.
